_meta.source: job_description.md - Senior AI Engineer, Founding Team @ Redrob AI
_meta.extraction_method: manual analysis (static, one-time, no LLM call needed at runtime or build-time)
_meta.note: This file is the structured decomposition of the JD. It is hardcoded and checked into the repo so that runtime never needs network access or an LLM call to re-derive it. See precompute/jd_requirements.py for an optional Gemini-based regeneration path if the JD ever changes.

experience_range.note: A soft band, not a hard cutoff. Strong candidates slightly outside this range should still be considered if other signals are strong.

ideal_applied_ai_years.note: Of total experience, ideally 4-5 years specifically in applied ML/AI roles at PRODUCT companies, not pure services/consulting.

hard_requirements.description: Production experience with embeddings-based retrieval systems (sentence-transformers, OpenAI embeddings, BGE, E5, or similar) deployed to real users. Specific model doesn't matter; operational experience with embedding drift, index refresh, retrieval-quality regression does.

Production experience with vector databases or hybrid search infra (Pinecone, Weaviate, Qdrant, Milvus, OpenSearch, Elasticsearch, FAISS, or similar). Specific tech doesn't matter; operational experience does.
Hands-on experience designing evaluation frameworks for ranking systems - NDCG, MRR, MAP, offline-to-online correlation, A/B test interpretation.

pure_research_no_production.description: Career spent entirely in pure research environments (academic labs, research-only roles) without ANY production deployment.
pure_research_no_production.detection_hint: Check career_history for academic/research-lab employers + absence of 'deployed', 'production', 'shipped', 'served', 'users' language in descriptions.

recent_llm_only_no_pre_llm_ml.description: AI experience consists primarily of recent (<12 months) LangChain+OpenAI projects, with no substantial pre-LLM-era ML production experience.
recent_llm_only_no_pre_llm_ml.detection_hint: Total years_of_experience is high but ML/AI-relevant career history is short (<12mo) AND skills are dominated by LangChain/OpenAI wrapper tools rather than fundamentals (embeddings, ranking, retrieval, classical ML).

no_recent_production_code.description: Senior engineer who hasn't written production code in 18+ months due to moving into pure 'architecture'/'tech lead' roles. This role writes code.
no_recent_production_code.detection_hint: Most recent job title contains 'Architect', 'Tech Lead', 'Engineering Manager', 'Director' etc. AND duration_months of that role >= 18 AND description lacks hands-on coding language.

title_chaser_job_hopper.description: Career trajectory shows optimizing for Senior->Staff->Principal titles by switching companies every ~1.5 years (18 months). They need someone planning to stay 3+ years.
title_chaser_job_hopper.detection_hint: avg_tenure_months across career_history < 18, especially combined with consistently escalating seniority words in titles.

framework_enthusiast_demo_builder.description: GitHub full of framework tutorials, blog posts are 'How I built X with [hot framework]' - i.e. demo-builder, not systems thinker.
framework_enthusiast_demo_builder.detection_hint: Hard to detect from structured fields alone; weak signal from skills being framework-name-heavy (LangChain, AutoGPT, CrewAI) without any production/scale/systems language in career descriptions.

consulting_only_career.description: Entire career spent at consulting/services firms (TCS, Infosys, Wipro, Accenture, Cognizant, Capgemini, etc). Currently AT one of these is fine IF prior product-company experience exists.
consulting_only_career.detection_hint: ALL companies in career_history match the consulting list -> hard flag. If only the CURRENT company matches but prior companies don't -> not a disqualifier.

cv_speech_robotics_without_nlp.description: Primary expertise is computer vision, speech, or robotics WITHOUT significant NLP/IR exposure. Respected work, but wrong fundamentals for this role.
cv_speech_robotics_without_nlp.detection_hint: Heavy presence of CV/Speech/Robotics keywords AND near-absence of NLP/IR keywords across skills + career descriptions.

closed_source_only_no_external_validatio.description: 5+ years entirely on closed-source proprietary systems with no external validation (no papers, talks, open-source contributions).
closed_source_only_no_external_validatio.detection_hint: years_of_experience >= 5 AND github_activity_score == -1 (no GitHub linked) AND no certifications/languages suggesting external engagement. This is a WEAK proxy signal since the schema doesn't have a direct 'papers/talks' field - github_activity_score is the best available proxy.

location.outside_india: case-by-case, no visa sponsorship - treat as a soft negative unless willing_to_relocate=true
location.work_mode: Hybrid (Noida/Pune offices, Pune mostly Tue/Thu), quarterly travel for offsites expected
location.remote_only_preference_penalty: soft negative - role expects in-person presence, not fully remote

notice_period.note: No candidates in the sample had <30 days, so this MUST be a soft scoring penalty that scales with days, not a hard filter.

behavioral_emphasis.note: JD explicitly instructs: a perfect-on-paper candidate who hasn't logged in for 6 months and has a 5% recruiter response rate is NOT actually available for hiring purposes. Down-weight such candidates appropriately. This directly maps to using redrob_signals as a MULTIPLIER on top of skill-match score, not a separate additive component.

explicit_trap_warning.keyword_stuffing_trap: A candidate with all the AI keywords listed as skills but whose title/career is e.g. 'Marketing Manager' is NOT a fit, regardless of how complete their skill list looks.
explicit_trap_warning.plain_language_tier5_trap: A candidate may never use words like 'RAG' or 'Pinecone' but if career history shows they built a recommendation system at a product company, they ARE a fit. Semantic understanding of career_history.description must catch this, not keyword matching.
explicit_trap_warning.design_implication: current_title and skills[].name are SECONDARY, weaker signals. profile.summary and career_history[].description (deduplicated) are PRIMARY signals for semantic fit.

ideal_candidate_summary.has_shipped: at least one end-to-end ranking/search/recommendation system to real users at meaningful scale
ideal_candidate_summary.location_fit: in or willing to relocate to Noida/Pune
ideal_candidate_summary.reachability: active on platform or clear signal of being in job market

scoring_weight_hints.note: Suggested relative emphasis for Phase 2 composite scoring, derived from how much weight the JD itself places on each dimension. Final weights tuned in ranker/scoring.py.
scoring_weight_hints.semantic_career_fit: highest priority - this is what 50% of NDCG@10 will reward if done well
scoring_weight_hints.production_experience: very high - explicitly called out twice in JD as a hard requirement
scoring_weight_hints.evaluation_framework_experience: high - explicitly named as a hard requirement, fairly rare signal
scoring_weight_hints.career_trajectory_stability: medium-high - explicit disqualifier for job-hoppers and title-chasers
scoring_weight_hints.behavioral_reachability: high - JD explicitly instructs to down-weight unreachable candidates
scoring_weight_hints.logistics_fit: medium - location/notice period matter but JD says 'flexible' and 'case-by-case' repeatedly, so should not dominate
